topic: kick(ω-cycle) carry-forward (a-live): verify the 3-condition freshness guard at tool/kick_bench.hexa:485-496 actually rejects stale witnesses on rc==0 paths and only counts fresh witnesses on real runs. Three target scenarios (S1 all-FAIL backends, S2 pre-seeded stale witness on a real backend, S3 clean small live topic). Hive infra obstruction surfaced; a synthetic alternative (kick_bench_freshness_smoke.hexa) exercises the guard mechanics directly and uncovered a latent macOS time-source bug that silently bypassed the guard in production.
rule: raw 44: SID via helper, never fabricated
honesty: NO paint-over (OBSTRUCTION_DOCUMENTED accepted as honest)

core_5_op_kernel.1_intake_topic: Confirm the freshness guard (status=='fire' AND non-empty witness_path AND mtime_s>=floor(t0/1000)) correctly REJECTS stale witnesses (status reassigned to 'fail-stale-witness', counter NOT incremented) and ONLY counts fresh witnesses, on actual live runs. Companion landing tool/hive_parent_sid.hexa was supposed to make live spawn possible.

3_fan_out_axes.rationale: The witnesses_landed counter must reflect ONLY iterations where (a) backend reported success (rc==0), (b) a witness path was actually emitted, and (c) the witness was written within the iteration's time window. Any path where the counter increments based on stale on-disk artifacts is a raw#80 sentinel honesty violation — the orchestrator would silently paint over all-FAIL bench runs as PASS.

The guard logic at lines 488-496 is structurally correct: status=='fire' && fresh → increment; status=='fire' && !fresh → reassign to fail-stale-witness. Time-source correctness is a precondition: t0_ms must be a real ms-epoch, otherwise t0_s=0 makes every existing file appear fresh and silently bypasses the guard.

4_tier_1_promotion_gate_via_falsifier_pa.promotion_criterion: F1 + F2 + F3 PASS required (honesty-critical); F4/F5/F6 strengthen but are not gate.
4_tier_1_promotion_gate_via_falsifier_pa.result: F1 PASS observed; F2 PASS observed; F3 PASS via synthetic harness (live path obstructed); F4 PASS via synthetic harness (live path obstructed); F5 PASS observed; F6 PASS observed. Honesty-critical gate met.

Without a witness_path token reaching kick_bench, the stale-on-fire branch (L494-496) cannot be exercised end-to-end.

obstruction_documented.scope: blocks live S2/S3 end-to-end verification through subagent_dispatch; does NOT affect the in-process freshness guard logic (which is what we are verifying). The synthetic harness fully exercises that logic without depending on live spawn.
obstruction_documented.honesty_note: Per kick policy: live spawn is a SEPARATE concern from the freshness guard. The honesty-critical question is whether the guard is correct — verified PASS via synthetic harness AND validated by behavioral observation in S1/S1b (counter stayed at 0 when no fresh witness was emitted). NO paint-over: S2/S3 live verdicts are OBSTRUCTION_DOCUMENTED, not PASS.

secondary_finding_macos_time_source_bug.severity: high — silently bypasses the freshness guard on macOS
secondary_finding_macos_time_source_bug.discovered_during: synthetic harness construction (the harness itself initially failed C2 with t0_ms=-5000, t0_s=-5)
secondary_finding_macos_time_source_bug.root_cause: tool/kick_bench.hexa's _now_unix_ms() ran `exec("date +%s%3N 2>/dev/null || python3 -c '…'")`. On macOS, BSD date(1) does NOT support %3N; it emits literal '<unix-seconds>N' (e.g. '17772453513N'). Because the BSD date EXITS 0 (it just outputs a malformed string), the OR-fallback `|| python3 ...` is never taken. The string then fails to_int (catch → v=0). Result: _now_unix_ms()==0 → t0_s=0 → every non-empty witness's mtime_s>=0 is true → every witness appears 'fresh' → freshness guard silently bypassed.
secondary_finding_macos_time_source_bug.evidence: `echo $(date +%s%3N)` → '17772453513N'; `hexa run /tmp/parse_probe.hexa` → 'threw: error: to_int: trailing garbage in "17772453513N" / v=-1' (caught → 0).
secondary_finding_macos_time_source_bug.blast_radius: all macOS environments running the kick_bench orchestrator after the 2026-04-26 freshness-guard fix would have the guard non-functional. Counter could still false-PASS on stale witnesses (the very thing the guard was meant to prevent). Linux unaffected (GNU date supports %3N).
secondary_finding_macos_time_source_bug.fix_applied: tool/kick_bench.hexa _now_unix_ms() now: try BSD date → to_int (catches → 0) → if v<=0 fall through to python3 ms → if v<=0 fall through to `date +%s` * 1000. Same fix applied to tool/kick_bench_freshness_smoke.hexa.
secondary_finding_macos_time_source_bug.post_fix_evidence: synthetic harness with corrected _now_unix_ms: t0_ms=1777245391084, t0_s=1777245391, fresh_path mtime_s=1777245395 (>=t0_s → fresh), stale_path mtime_s=946652400 (<t0_s → stale). All 5 cases PASS (5/5).

cases.want: status=fail, delta=0 (no reassignment when not fire)

tier_1_promotions.rationale: Time-source helpers in hexa tooling must explicitly cascade BSD-date → python3 → GNU date with rc-AND-content checks, not rely on shell `||` short-circuit. BSD date(1) exits 0 on unsupported format strings (just emits the literal), so `||` never triggers. Pattern reusable: any helper that needs ms-precision time (or any precision the BSD utility lacks) should use try/catch around to_int and an explicit `if v<=0 then fallback` cascade, not depend on exit-code chaining.
tier_1_promotions.evidence: tool/kick_bench.hexa _now_unix_ms() before fix collapsed to 0 on macOS → t0_s=0 → freshness guard non-functional. Post-fix cascade: BSD date → catch → if v<=0 python3 → if v<=0 GNU-style date → verified t0_ms=1777245391084 on macOS.

tier_1_promotions.rationale: When live infrastructure (hive subagent_dispatch / docker-routed hexa) is unavailable, a hexa-only synthetic harness that imports the SAME boolean logic verbatim is honest verification — not a paint-over — provided (a) the harness reproduces the exact predicate from the production code, (b) the obstruction is documented verbatim, and (c) the live verdict is OBSTRUCTION_DOCUMENTED rather than PASS-by-association. This is the raw#43 hard-implementation-default applied to verification: implement the verifier itself when the real path is blocked.
tier_1_promotions.evidence: tool/kick_bench_freshness_smoke.hexa _apply_guard mirrors kick_bench.hexa:488-496 verbatim; 5/5 cases PASS; live S2/S3 separately marked OBSTRUCTION_DOCUMENTED with verbatim hive error.

S1 all-FAIL run: stdout '__KICK_BENCH__ FAIL backends=2 witnesses=0', stderr 'reason=partial-witness-landing fix=tail …'; counter correctly 0; NOT painted as PASS

synthetic harness C2: input=(rc=0, stale witness mtime=946652400, t0_s=1777245391), got status='fail-stale-witness' delta=0 — mirrors kick_bench.hexa:488-496 boolean logic verbatim. Live-orchestrator path obstructed (see S2 verdict); guard logic itself verified correct
synthetic harness C1: input=(rc=0, fresh witness mtime>=t0_s), got status='fire' delta=1 — counter increments correctly. Live-orchestrator path obstructed (see S3 verdict)
post-_now_unix_ms-fix dry-run: '… dedup_ratio_vs_prior=0.0' on all 3 backends; numeric, not 'n/a'
S1 stderr: 'reason=partial-witness-landing fix=tail /Users/ghost/core/nexus/state/kick/bench/….jsonl; check per-backend status field' — raw 66 reason=<slug> fix=<directive> trailer present
